messages.content: Grade Sprint 11 Round 1 against the approved contract.

Read contract, tasks.json, config.json, dogfood-findings.md, and eval-harness.md rubric in parallel. Wrote evaluation skeleton. Ran all deterministic verification commands. Applied LLM-judge rubric to J10 and J11. Identified synthetic authoring as root cause of J10/J11 failures.

tool_calls.name: Read
tool_calls.result_summary: Full contract read including B1-B6, S7-S9, J10-J11, SN1-SN5 criteria

tool_calls.name: Read
tool_calls.result_summary: 229-line report; synthetic authoring disclosed in Section 1

tool_calls.name: Read
tool_calls.result_summary: 5 dimensions, thresholds at methodology_completeness>=3 and generator_evaluator_separation>=3

tool_calls.name: Bash
tool_calls.arguments_summary: 7 greps for B4 dimensions (Control Plane, Tool Registry, Projection, Skills, Observation, External Affordances, Governance)

tool_calls.name: Bash
tool_calls.arguments_summary: B6 two sub-conditions: broad verdict grep and section-header grep

tool_calls.name: Bash
tool_calls.arguments_summary: git diff HEAD -- sprint-07..10 contracts (4 diffs)

tool_calls.name: Bash
tool_calls.arguments_summary: grep -c '<!-- Context scope at this step:' harness-kickoff/SKILL.md

tool_calls.name: Bash
tool_calls.arguments_summary: git diff HEAD for planner.md, harness-build.md, harness-build.json

tool_calls.name: Read
tool_calls.result_summary: Harness-Build Mode section confirms playbook_stage emission, stage ordering, backward compat

thinking_summary: The primary challenge in this eval was the synthetic authoring disclosure in dogfood-findings.md Section 1. Every deterministic criterion (B2-B6, S7-S9, all gates) passed because they test properties of the report document, not execution. The LLM-judge criteria J10 and J11 failed because J10(b) explicitly disqualifies synthetic examples, and J11(a) cannot be confirmed when no planner subagent ran. The resist-rationalization rule was applied: the artifacts look correct and the report is well-structured, but the contract's evidence standard is clear. I scored J10 FAIL rather than talking myself into 'close enough.' The rubric dimensions were scored in isolation: methodology_completeness 3/5 (core loop form present, execution missing), grading_architecture 3/5 (behavioral criteria measure report not execution), generator_evaluator_separation 3/5 (borderline — Generator authored what it expected the system to produce), context_engineering 4/5 (JIT scoping was done well), extensibility_aci 3/5 (calibration signals present but from synthetic data). Both critical dimensions meet the minimum 3/5 threshold, so the rubric does not trigger an automatic block — the PARTIAL verdict comes from the contract criteria failures.
